section_path: High Risk Status > HR2: Annex I Section A categories
content:
## Decision question: Does your AI system fall within any of the high-risk product categories listed in Annex I, Section A?

Section A of Annex I lists Union harmonisation legislation for product categories where the AI Act's high-risk regime interacts directly with existing third-party conformity assessment requirements. Falling within a Section A category does not by itself trigger high-risk classification; it triggers a further test (HR3) on whether the product is required to undergo a third-party conformity assessment under the relevant sectoral law.

The Section A categories are:

- Machinery
- Toys
- Recreational craft and personal watercraft
- Lifts and safety components of lifts
- Equipment and protective systems intended for use in potentially explosive atmospheres
- Radio equipment
- Pressure equipment
- Cableway installations
- Personal protective equipment
- Appliances burning gaseous fuels
- Medical devices
- In vitro diagnostic medical devices

If the AI system falls within any of these categories, the assessment proceeds to the third-party conformity assessment question (HR3), which determines whether the product is on the regulated path that triggers high-risk status under Article 6 point 1.

If the AI system does not fall within any Section A category, the assessment proceeds to the Annex III test (HR4), which covers stand-alone high-risk use cases not tied to product harmonisation legislation.

Source: Article 6 point 1, Annex I Section A
source_document: FLI EU AI Act Compliance Checker
